[Repo Assist] Fix HtmlElement SVG serialisation: LinearGradient/RadialGradient emitted invalid tag `(radient)` (#1108)

* Fix HtmlElement SVG serialisation: LinearGradient and RadialGradient emitted wrong tag name 'radient'

Both LinearGradient and RadialGradient cases in HtmlElement.ToString() were
passing the literal string "radient" to the format helper instead of
"linearGradient" and "radialGradient" respectively. This meant that any
SVG element using these types would render as the invalid tag <radient> in
generated HTML.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] test: expand Markdown.ToMd round-trip coverage with 15 new tests (#1127)

* test: expand ToMd round-trip coverage with 15 new tests

Add tests for heading levels 4-6, emphasis/strong inside headings,
LaTeX display math, nested blockquotes, emphasis/code inside blockquotes,
code blocks without language, and link titles.  All 281 markdown tests
pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* fix: ToMd now serialises single-line equation blocks as $$...$$ not \begin{equation}

Markdown.ToMd previously serialised LatexBlock paragraphs (i.e. those
written as `$$body$$` or `$$$...$$$`) using the LaTeX environment form:

    \begin{equation}
    body
    \end{equation}

This broke the round-trip for the common `$$...$$` notation and caused
the 'ToMd preserves LaTeX display math' test to fail.

Fix: when the environment is 'equation' and the body is a single line,
emit the compact `$$body$$` form instead. Multi-line bodies and
non-equation environments keep the `\begin{env}...\end{env}` form.

All 295 markdown tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

Replace the verbose 'Results: X passed, Y failed, Z errors, N total' line
with a concise, pytest-style summary:

- Clean runs: '42 passed in 1.8s'
- Runs with issues: '9 passed, 2 failed, 3 blocked (runner limitation) in 1.8s'

Changes:
- Executor.PrintResults now accepts optional totalMs parameter
- 'errors' renamed to 'blocked (runner limitation)' for clarity
- Elapsed time tracked in Pipeline.cs via Stopwatch around RunTests()
- Four new unit tests in RunnerErrorClassificationTests.cs covering all cases

* Fix member template variadic pack expansion in constructor calls

- Add variadic pack parameter handling to instantiate_member_function_template_core
  to expand Args... parameters into individual args_0, args_1, etc. parameters
- Detect variadic template parameters by checking if the parameter type name
  matches a variadic template parameter (handles cases where is_parameter_pack
  flag isn't set)
- Set up pack_param_info_ for body parsing to enable pack expansion
- Add pack expansion support in constructor argument parsing
  (parse_expression followed by ...) to expand args... into individual args
- Restore pack_param_info_ at function end to avoid polluting outer context

This enables member function templates with variadic parameters like:
  template <typename... Args>
  void emplace(Args&&... args) { value = T(args...); }
to be properly instantiated and have their bodies parsed correctly.

fix: detect empty GitHub releases and propagate exit code on failure (#57)

* chore: update DotnetPackaging packages to 10.1.7

Update all DotnetPackaging package references from 10.1.0 to 10.1.7.
Flatpak stays at 9.0.6 (removed from DotnetPackaging in v10.0.0).

Notable upstream changes in 10.1.1–10.1.7:
- Improved error handling in DotnetPublisher (better build error extraction)
- RPM packaging fixes (AppStream metadata, URI parsing, header structure)
- IsTerminal changed to Maybe<bool> (inferred from OutputType)
- systemd service/daemon support for .deb packages
- from-directory subcommand in CLI tool

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: detect empty GitHub releases and propagate exit code on failure

When all package builds fail, the deployer now:
- Deletes the empty release instead of leaving it on GitHub
- Cleans up the release if an exception occurs during upload
- Returns a non-zero exit code so CI pipelines fail correctly

The exit code bug was caused by SetAction(Func<ParseResult, Task>) which
ignores Environment.ExitCode. Now uses a closure variable returned from Main.

commit.message:
feat(engagement): flow detector — pure 2-state light/deep detection primitive (#141)

* feat(engagement): flow detector service (ENG-02)

Pure computational service that detects writing flow state from keystroke
timing and window focus events.

Algorithm:
- Sliding window of keystroke timestamps, bounded to retention window
- Continuous typing (gaps ≤ 30s) for 5 min → light flow
- Continuous typing for 15 min → deep flow
- Window blur → immediate flow break
- 60s silence → exit flow and reset

Key design decisions:
- Zero dependencies — pure computation, no DB/LLM/IPC
- All time values injectable via parameters (INV P4 deterministic testing)
- Keystroke buffer auto-pruned to deepThreshold + exitTimeout window
- O(n) backward scan on bounded buffer → O(1) amortized getFlowState
- Blur uses latest keystroke timestamp as chain-break marker (not
  Date.now()) to stay in the same time domain as injected timestamps

Coverage: 95% statements, 89% branches, 100% functions (18 tests)

Closes #140

* fix: address R1 audit findings for flow detector

- Change maxKeystrokeGapMs default from 30s to 15s (spec: IN_FLOW 停顿<15s)
- Fix false spec citation in flowExitTimeoutMs comment
- Fix blur boundary bug: latest keystroke now checked against lastBlurTime
- Add monotonic timestamp enforcement in recordKeystroke
- Filter future keystrokes in getFlowState (now < latest)
- Update test 14 gap from 25s to 10s, test 17 description 30s→15s
- Add 8 new edge case tests: blur before input, out-of-order timestamps,
  duplicate timestamps, future keystroke filtering, blur boundary checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address R2 audit findings — maxGap boundary and docs

- Change > to >= for maxGap comparisons (3 sites) to match spec
  'IN_FLOW: 停顿 < 15 秒' — gap of exactly maxGap now breaks chain
- Update maxKeystrokeGapMs JSDoc to clarify >= semantics
- Clarify getFlowState now param: keystroke filtering only, not blur/focus
- Replace misleading boundary test with proper gap==maxGap + gap==maxGap-1
- Update test header comment (24 tests, 15s gap)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(flow-detector): R3→R4 — track activeChainStart for accurate duration beyond retention window

- Add activeChainStart state to track true chain start across buffer pruning
- Fix blur sentinel from 0 to -1 to avoid timestamp-0 collision
- Fix stale comments: ≤ maxGap → < maxGap, > maxGap → >= maxGap
- Update rapid-fire test to expect full duration (not capped by retention)
- Add 3 regression tests: pruning duration, timestamp-0 post-blur, blur chain reset
- Total: 28 tests all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(flow-detector): R4→R5 — guard recordKeystroke against blur, fix exit timeout boundary

- Add windowFocused guard in recordKeystroke: ignore keystrokes while blurred
- Change exit timeout from > to >= for boundary consistency with maxGap
- Fix stale test comment: lastBlurTime = 0 → -1 (sentinel)
- Add 3 regression tests: blurred keystrokes ignored, post-focus re-entry,
  exit timeout at exact boundary
- Total: 31 tests all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(flow-detector): R5→R6 — fix stale comments and playbook doc

R5 audit findings (all comment/doc accuracy):
- Fix 'circular buffer' misnomer → 'Bounded array' (Opus+Sonnet)
- Fix 'binary-search-like' → 'linear forward scan' (Opus+Sonnet)
- Fix config comment 'resets state' → 'reports no-flow' (Duck)
- Fix test header 'gap > 15s' → 'gap >= 15s' (Duck)
- Fix playbook doc: 30s → 15s, '> 60s' → '>= 60s' (Duck)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: validate exitTimeout >= maxGap to prevent chain revival

Duck R6 finding: when flowExitTimeoutMs < maxKeystrokeGapMs, getFlowState
exits flow after exitTimeout silence, but recordKeystroke does not break
the chain (gap still < maxGap). A later keystroke could then revive the
old chain and instantly report flow — semantically contradictory.

Fix: throw at construction time if exitTimeout < maxGap. Also fix the
existing exit-timeout-boundary test to satisfy the new invariant
(set maxGap = exitTimeout instead of maxGap > exitTimeout).

Added: 2 new tests (config validation: rejects invalid, accepts equal).
Tests: 33/33 green.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: clarify exitTimeout/maxGap relationship, fix test count and message

R7 fixes (all 3 seats):
- Opus/Sonnet NIT: test header '31 tests' → '33 tests', add validation bullet
- Duck blocking: clarify that exitTimeout is shadowed by the tighter maxGap
  check in the current 2-state model (spec: 停顿 < 15 秒). exitTimeout is
  retained as upper bound for the planned multi-state model (STUCK_PAUSE/
  COOLING from §机制8). Updated JSDoc on FlowDetectorConfig and @why
  comments in getFlowState.
- Duck non-blocking: test now asserts full error message string

Tests: 33/33 green. Zero logic changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: playbook exit threshold 60s → 15s (maxGap is effective exit)

Duck R8 finding: playbook line 47 said '检测到 >= 60s 无输入' but the
effective exit in the current 2-state model is maxGap (15s), not
exitTimeout (60s). Updated to accurately describe the current behavior
and note that exitTimeout is reserved for the planned multi-state model.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: playbook ENG-02 behavior — pure detector, Zen Mode is downstream

Duck R9 finding: playbook line 46 said 'Zen Mode 自动触发' but this PR
only ships the flow detection primitive. Updated to clarify it's a pure
detector (read-only FlowState, no UI/IPC/events) and Zen Mode integration
is downstream (renderer consumes FlowState).

* fix: handle EmbedParagraphs in Markdown.ToMd serialiser

Previously EmbedParagraphs fell through to the catch-all '| _' branch in
MarkdownUtils.formatParagraph, emitting a debug printfn to stdout and
yielding an empty string.  All other formatters (HTML, LaTeX) delegate
to cmd.Render() and recurse; this commit brings the Markdown-to-Markdown
back-end into line with them.

The now-dead catch-all branch is removed so the exhaustiveness checker
will catch any new MarkdownParagraph cases that are added without a
corresponding formatParagraph clause.

whenever a document containing EmbedParagraphs was serialised via

commit.message:
perf: keep event loop free during backfill stats import

- aggregate_readings() is now run in a thread pool worker via
  asyncio.to_thread() so the CPU-heavy aggregation loop does not
  block the event loop while the SSE task is processing live data.
- Added await asyncio.sleep(0) between PID iterations in
  import_statistics() so the event loop can service SSE and
  health-poll tasks during large backfill imports.
- Added two tests: one verifying asyncio.to_thread is used for
  aggregation, one verifying the per-PID yield (sleep(0) × N PIDs).

    plus a combined textContent blob for whole-section RAG indexing
  - Noise filter removes font names, GUIDs, XML fragments, and
    OneNote property keys from extracted text

- New IPC handler: onedrive:read-onenote-file
  - Accepts a full file path, validates .one extension
  - Returns { ok, section } or { ok: false, error }

- Types: OneNotePageContent and OneNoteSectionContent added to
  plugins/types.ts; onedriveReadOneNoteFile added to JarvisApi

- Preload: onedriveReadOneNoteFile exposed via contextBridge

- Tests: 19 new tests using fixture .one files from the installed
  @joplin/onenote-converter package (no binary blobs committed)

* test: add OneNote folder integration tests

Proves the full group→OneDrive discovery→file scan→content extraction
pipeline using the @joplin/onenote-converter fixture files as a stand-in
OneDrive root.

- Sets up an in-memory DB group named to match a fixture subdirectory
- Runs discoverCustomerFolderForGroup, scanFilesForFolder, listFilesForFolder
- Reads every .one file found via readOneNoteSection
- Logs a formatted section/page summary (visible with --reporter=verbose)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add OneNote section viewer panel in Groups UI

When a OneDrive folder has .one files, each file now shows a 📖 button
in the expanded file list. Clicking it opens a OneNoteSectionPanel to
the right of GroupsPanel showing the extracted content.

- OneNoteSectionPanel: reads the file via onedriveReadOneNoteFile IPC,
  shows section name + page count in the header, then a card per page
  with title (heading), date, and body text (expandable past 320 chars)
- GroupsPanel: accepts onOpenOneNote callback, renders 📓 icon + 📖 button
  for .one files; constructs full path from folderPath + relativePath
- index.tsx: wires up oneNoteFilePath state, passes onOpenOneNote prop,
  renders OneNoteSectionPanel in groups-layout; clears on panel close

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: read and open .url Internet Shortcut files in file list

OneNote notebooks hosted on SharePoint are represented locally as .url
shortcut files instead of .one files. This adds first-class support:

- New service src/services/url-shortcut.ts: parses the INI-format .url
  file and returns { url, isOneNote, isSharePoint }
- New IPC handler onedrive:read-url-shortcut: reads .url file -> URL
- New IPC handler shell:open-url: opens a validated https:/onenote: URL
  in the system browser/OneNote app
- GroupsPanel: pre-loads .url info when a folder expands; shows
  notebook+globe icon for OneNote links, link icon for other URLs,
  SharePoint badge, and an Open button that launches the URL

Content extraction requires Microsoft Graph API (notebook lives on
SharePoint, not locally synced). The open button is the practical
workaround until Graph API auth is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use URL hostname parsing instead of substring check for SharePoint

CodeQL flagged incomplete URL substring sanitization: using .includes()
allows spoofing via paths or subdomains (e.g. evil.com/sharepoint.com).

Replace with proper URL parsing: check parsed.hostname equals or ends
with '.sharepoint.com' to safely identify SharePoint-hosted links.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves DirectLink and DirectImage titles (#1150)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves DirectLink and DirectImage titles

When serialising a document with ToMd, link and image titles were
silently dropped.  A Markdown fragment such as

    [text](url "My Title")

would round-trip as

    [text](url)

because the `DirectLink` / `DirectImage` formatSpan cases matched the
title field with a wildcard.

Fix: give DirectLink and DirectImage their own branches so the optional
title can be appended in CommonMark form (  \"title\").

Three new round-trip tests cover:
  - DirectLink with title
  - DirectLink without title (unchanged behaviour)
  - DirectImage with title

All 284 Markdown tests pass.

commit.message:
Add dhcpmon v6 counter CLI (show/clear) and update tests (#26467)

show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters: repurposed to show dhcpmon v6 counters from COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB wrapper)
clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters: repurposed to clear dhcpmon v6 counters via SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 protocol (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB)
Merged duplicate v4/v6 dhcpmon functions into shared parameterized versions (prefix parameter for table selection)
Fixed v4 clear to include Malformed/Ignored counter types
Added section separators and comments for code organization
Old dhcp6relay commands still available via standalone commands: show dhcp6relay_counters counts / sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters
Updated unit tests for renamed functions
Breaking Changes
show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters now reads dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB). Old dhcp6relay counters remain accessible via show dhcp6relay_counters counts. dhcpmon v6 ships in the same image, so this command will always have data.

clear dhcp_relay ipv4 counters now also zeros "Malformed" and "Ignored" counter types. This is intentional — dhcpmon writes these types to COUNTERS_DB, and not zeroing them during the SIGUSR2 cache-sync causes stale values to persist after clear.

clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters now clears dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB (was dhcp6relay STATE_DB). Old dhcp6relay clear remains accessible via sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters.

Design Notes
SIGUSR signal scope: There is one dhcpmon process per VLAN handling both v4 and v6. SIGUSR1/SIGUSR2 pauses all counter writes (both protocols) which is necessary for consistent clear.
Show/clear type asymmetry: show intentionally excludes Malformed/Ignored from display to keep output clean, but clear must zero all types (including Malformed/Ignored) for the SIGUSR cache-sync protocol to work correctly.
Why I did it
sonic-dhcpmon now supports DHCPv6 counters (PR sonic-dhcpmon#62), writing to
DHCPV6_COUNTER_TABLE in COUNTERS_DB. The CLI needs matching show/clear commands
so operators can view and reset these counters, and so the test infrastructure
can validate them.

Work item tracking
Microsoft ADO (number only):
How I did it
sonic-dhcpmon added DHCPv6 counter support (sonic-dhcpmon#62), writing per-interface v6 packet counts to DHCPV6_COUNTER_TABLE in COUNTERS_DB. However, the CLI had no commands to display or clear these v6 counters. The existing show/clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters commands were thin wrappers around dhcp6relay STATE_DB data, which is a separate counter
source with less granularity (no per-interface breakdown, no per-direction TX/RX).

This PR repurposes those commands to show/clear dhcpmon COUNTERS_DB data instead, giving operators consistent v4/v6 counter visibility through a single CLI interface. The old dhcp6relay counters remain accessible via standalone commands (show dhcp6relay_counters counts / sonic-clear dhcp6relay_counters).

How to verify it
Deploy dhcpmon with v6 support on a DUT with DHCPv6 relay configured.
Run show dhcp_relay ipv6 counters — should display per-VLAN/per-interface
DHCPv6 packet counts from COUNTERS_DB.
Run sonic-clear dhcp_relay ipv6 counters — should zero all v6 counters.
Run show dhcp6relay_counters counts — should still show dhcp6relay
STATE_DB counters independently.
Existing v4 commands (show/clear dhcp_relay ipv4 counters) unchanged in behavior.
Unit tests: cd dockers/docker-dhcp-relay/cli-plugin-tests && pytest
